119: 	if data.Branch == "" {
120: 		data.Branch = currentBranch(m.activeRepoPath)
121: 	}
122: 
123: 	return data
124: }
125: 
126: // currentBranch returns the name of the currently checked-out branch in repoPath.
127: // Falls back to "main" if the branch cannot be determined (e.g. detached HEAD).
128: func currentBranch(repoPath string) string {
129: 	cmd := exec.Command("git", "branch", "--show-current")
130: 	cmd.Dir = repoPath
131: 	out, err := cmd.Output()
132: 	if err != nil {
133: 		return "main"
134: 	}
135: 	branch := strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
136: 	if branch == "" {
137: 		return "main" // detached HEAD
138: 	}
139: 	return branch
140: }
141: 
142: // computePlanStatuses builds per-plan instance status flags (running/notification)
143: // from the current instance list.
144: func (m *home) computePlanStatuses() map[string]ui.TopicStatus {
145: 	planStatuses := make(map[string]ui.TopicStatus)
146: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
147: 		if inst.TaskFile == "" {
148: 			continue
149: 		}
150: 		planSt := planStatuses[inst.TaskFile]
151: 		planStatuses[inst.TaskFile] = mergePlanStatus(planSt, inst, inst.Started())
152: 	}
153: 	return planStatuses
154: }
155: 
156: // updateNavPanelStatus recomputes plan instance statuses and triggers a row
157: // rebuild. Use this after instance mutations (kill, remove, pause) where the
158: // plan list itself hasn't changed. When updateSidebarTasks is also called,
159: // skip this — updateSidebarTasks already includes plan statuses in its rebuild.
160: func (m *home) updateNavPanelStatus() {
161: 	m.nav.SetItems(nil, nil, 0, nil, nil, m.computePlanStatuses())
162: }

164: // focusSlot constants for readability.
165: // Order matches tab layout: info (first), agent (second), diff (third).
166: const (
167: 	slotNav   = 0
168: 	slotInfo  = 1
169: 	slotAgent = 2
170: 	slotDiff  = 3
171: 	slotCount = 4
172: )
173: 
174: // setFocusSlot updates which pane has focus and syncs visual state.
175: func (m *home) setFocusSlot(slot int) {
176: 	m.focusSlot = slot
177: 	m.nav.SetFocused(slot == slotNav)
178: 	m.menu.SetFocusSlot(slot)
179: 
180: 	// Center pane is focused when any of the 3 center tabs is active.
181: 	centerFocused := slot >= slotInfo && slot <= slotDiff
182: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetFocused(centerFocused)
183: 
184: 	// When focusing a center tab, switch the visible tab to match and track which tab is focused.
185: 	if centerFocused {
186: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(slot - slotInfo) // slotInfo=1 → InfoTab=0, etc.
187: 	}
188: 	// focusedTab always tracks activeTab so the gradient header is visible
189: 	// regardless of which pane has keyboard focus.
190: }
191: 
192: // nextFocusSlot cycles the visible center tab forward (info → agent → diff → info).
193: // The sidebar always retains keyboard focus (focusSlot stays slotNav); only the
194: // displayed tab changes. This is called by Tab and →.
195: func (m *home) nextFocusSlot() {
196: 	switch m.tabbedWindow.GetActiveTab() {
197: 	case ui.InfoTab:
198: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.PreviewTab)
199: 	case ui.PreviewTab:
200: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.DiffTab)
201: 	default: // ui.DiffTab — wraps to info
202: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.InfoTab)
203: 	}
204: }
205: 
206: // prevFocusSlot cycles the visible center tab backward (info → diff → agent → info).
207: // The sidebar always retains keyboard focus (focusSlot stays slotNav).
208: func (m *home) prevFocusSlot() {
209: 	switch m.tabbedWindow.GetActiveTab() {
210: 	case ui.PreviewTab:
211: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.InfoTab)
212: 	case ui.DiffTab:
213: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.PreviewTab)
214: 	default: // ui.InfoTab — wraps to diff
215: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.DiffTab)
216: 	}
217: }
218: 
219: // enterFocusMode enters focus/insert mode and starts the fast preview ticker.
220: // enterFocusMode reuses the existing previewTerminal if it is already attached to
221: // the selected instance — entering focus just toggles key forwarding to the same
222: // terminal. Only spawns a new terminal if none is attached yet (rare fallback).
223: func (m *home) enterFocusMode() tea.Cmd {
224: 	m.tabbedWindow.ClearDocumentMode()
225: 	selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
226: 	if selected == nil || !selected.Started() || selected.Status == session.Paused {
227: 		return nil
228: 	}
229: 
230: 	// If previewTerminal is already attached to this instance, just enter focus mode.
231: 	if m.previewTerminal != nil && m.previewTerminalInstance == selected.Title {
232: 		m.state = stateFocusAgent
233: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetFocusMode(true)
234: 		m.menu.SetFocusMode(true)
235: 		return nil
236: 	}
237: 
238: 	// No terminal yet (shouldn't normally happen) — spawn one synchronously-ish.
239: 	cols, rows := m.tabbedWindow.GetPreviewSize()
240: 	if cols < 10 {
241: 		cols = 80
242: 	}
243: 	if rows < 5 {
244: 		rows = 24
245: 	}
246: 	term, err := selected.NewEmbeddedTerminalForInstance(cols, rows)
247: 	if err != nil {
248: 		return m.handleError(err)
249: 	}
250: 	m.previewTerminal = term
251: 	m.previewTerminalInstance = selected.Title
252: 	m.state = stateFocusAgent
253: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetFocusMode(true)
254: 	m.menu.SetFocusMode(true)
255: 
256: 	return nil
257: }
258: 
259: // exitFocusMode resets focus state. previewTerminal stays alive — it continues
260: // rendering in normal preview mode after the user exits focus/insert mode.
261: func (m *home) exitFocusMode() {
262: 	// previewTerminal stays alive — it continues rendering in normal preview mode.
263: 	m.state = stateDefault
264: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetFocusMode(false)
265: 	m.menu.SetFocusMode(false)
266: }
267: 
268: // switchToTab changes the visible center tab without stealing focus from the sidebar.
269: // The sidebar (slotNav) always retains keyboard focus.
270: func (m *home) switchToTab(name keys.KeyName) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
271: 	var targetTab int
272: 	switch name {
273: 	case keys.KeyTabAgent:
274: 		targetTab = ui.PreviewTab
275: 	case keys.KeyTabDiff:
276: 		targetTab = ui.DiffTab
277: 	case keys.KeyTabInfo:
278: 		targetTab = ui.InfoTab
279: 	default:
280: 		return m, nil
281: 	}
282: 
283: 	if m.tabbedWindow.GetActiveTab() == targetTab {
284: 		return m, nil
285: 	}
286: 
287: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(targetTab)
288: 	return m, nil
289: }
290: 
291: // saveAllInstances saves allInstances (all repos) to storage.
292: // No-ops gracefully when storage is nil (e.g. in unit tests).
293: func (m *home) saveAllInstances() error {
294: 	if m.storage == nil {
295: 		return nil
296: 	}
297: 	return m.storage.SaveInstances(m.allInstances)
298: }
299: 
300: // removeFromAllInstances removes an instance from the master list by title.
301: func (m *home) removeFromAllInstances(title string) {
302: 	for i, inst := range m.allInstances {
303: 		if inst.Title == title {
304: 			m.allInstances = append(m.allInstances[:i], m.allInstances[i+1:]...)
305: 			return
306: 		}
307: 	}
308: }
309: 
310: // instanceChanged updates the preview pane, menu, and diff pane based on the selected instance.
311: // It returns a tea.Cmd when an async operation is needed (terminal spawn).
312: func (m *home) instanceChanged() tea.Cmd {
313: 	// selected may be nil
314: 	selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
315: 
316: 	// Clear notification on the previously-viewed instance when the user
317: 	// navigates away. This prevents the item from jumping out of "attention"
318: 	// while the user is still looking at it.
319: 	if m.seenNotified != nil && m.seenNotified != selected {
320: 		m.seenNotified.Notified = false
321: 		m.seenNotified = nil
322: 		m.updateNavPanelStatus()
323: 	}
324: 	if selected != nil && selected.Notified {
325: 		m.seenNotified = selected
326: 	}
327: 
328: 	// Manage preview terminal lifecycle on selection change.
329: 	var spawnCmd tea.Cmd
330: 	if selected == nil || !selected.Started() || selected.Status == session.Paused || selected.Exited {
331: 		// No valid instance (or dead tmux session) — tear down terminal.
332: 		if m.previewTerminal != nil {
333: 			m.previewTerminal.Close()
334: 		}
335: 		m.previewTerminal = nil
336: 		m.previewTerminalInstance = ""
337: 	} else if selected.Title != m.previewTerminalInstance {
338: 		// Different instance selected — swap terminal.
339: 		if m.previewTerminal != nil {
340: 			m.previewTerminal.Close()
341: 		}
342: 		m.previewTerminal = nil
343: 		m.previewTerminalInstance = ""
344: 
345: 		cols, rows := m.tabbedWindow.GetPreviewSize()
346: 		if cols < 10 {
347: 			cols = 80
348: 		}
349: 		if rows < 5 {
350: 			rows = 24
351: 		}
352: 		capturedTitle := selected.Title
353: 		capturedInstance := selected
354: 		spawnCmd = func() tea.Msg {
355: 			term, err := capturedInstance.NewEmbeddedTerminalForInstance(cols, rows)
356: 			return previewTerminalReadyMsg{term: term, instanceTitle: capturedTitle, err: err}
357: 		}
358: 	}
359: 
360: 	m.tabbedWindow.UpdateDiff(selected)
361: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetInstance(selected)
362: 	m.updateInfoPane()
363: 	// Update menu with current instance
364: 	m.menu.SetInstance(selected)
365: 
366: 	// Auto-switch tab based on selection type: plan header → info tab, instance → agent tab.
367: 	// Only auto-switch when the nav panel is focused to avoid hijacking explicit tab selection.
368: 	if m.focusSlot == slotNav {
369: 		if selected != nil {
370: 			m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.PreviewTab)
371: 		} else if m.nav.IsSelectedPlanHeader() {
372: 			m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.InfoTab)
373: 		}
374: 	}
375: 
376: 	// Collect async commands.
377: 	var cmds []tea.Cmd
378: 	if spawnCmd != nil {
379: 		cmds = append(cmds, spawnCmd)
380: 	}
381: 
382: 	if len(cmds) == 0 {
383: 		return nil
384: 	}
385: 	if len(cmds) == 1 {
386: 		return cmds[0]
387: 	}
388: 	return tea.Batch(cmds...)
389: }
390: 
391: // focusInstanceForOverlay selects the given instance in the nav panel and
392: // switches the preview to show its output behind an overlay dialog. The user
393: // can see the agent's terminal behind the overlay to understand context before
394: // responding. Returns a tea.Cmd if an async terminal spawn is needed.
395: func (m *home) focusInstanceForOverlay(inst *session.Instance) tea.Cmd {
396: 	if inst == nil {
397: 		return nil
398: 	}
399: 	m.nav.SelectInstance(inst)
400: 	return m.instanceChanged()
401: }
402: 
403: // focusPlanInstanceForOverlay selects the best instance belonging to the given
404: // plan file, preferring running instances. This is used before showing overlay
405: // dialogs about plan lifecycle events (wave completion, etc.) so the user can
406: // see the agent output behind the overlay.
407: func (m *home) focusPlanInstanceForOverlay(planFile string) tea.Cmd {
408: 	var best *session.Instance
409: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
410: 		if inst.TaskFile != planFile {
411: 			continue
412: 		}
413: 		if best == nil {
414: 			best = inst
415: 		}
416: 		// Prefer running instances over ready/paused ones.
417: 		if inst.Status == session.Running && best.Status != session.Running {
418: 			best = inst
419: 		}
420: 	}
421: 	return m.focusInstanceForOverlay(best)
422: }
423: 
424: func statusString(s session.Status) string {
425: 	switch s {
426: 	case session.Running:
427: 		return "running"
428: 	case session.Ready:
429: 		return "ready"
430: 	case session.Loading:
431: 		return "loading"
432: 	case session.Paused:
433: 		return "paused"
434: 	default:
435: 		return "unknown"
436: 	}
437: }

439: // updateInfoPaneForPlanHeader populates the info tab when a plan header is selected
440: // (no instance). Shows plan metadata and instance summary counts.
441: func (m *home) updateInfoPaneForPlanHeader() {
442: 	planFile := m.nav.GetSelectedPlanFile()
443: 	if planFile == "" || m.taskState == nil {
444: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetInfoData(ui.InfoData{IsPlanHeaderSelected: true})
445: 		return
446: 	}
447: 	entry, ok := m.taskState.Entry(planFile)
448: 	if !ok {
449: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetInfoData(ui.InfoData{IsPlanHeaderSelected: true})
450: 		return
451: 	}
452: 	data := ui.InfoData{
453: 		IsPlanHeaderSelected: true,
454: 		PlanName:             taskstate.DisplayName(planFile),
455: 		PlanStatus:           string(entry.Status),
456: 		PlanTopic:            entry.Topic,
457: 		PlanBranch:           entry.Branch,
458: 	}
459: 	if !entry.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
460: 		data.PlanCreated = entry.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02")
461: 	}
462: 	// Count instances belonging to this plan.
463: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
464: 		if inst.TaskFile != planFile {
465: 			continue
466: 		}
467: 		data.PlanInstanceCount++
468: 		switch {
469: 		case inst.Status == session.Running || inst.Status == session.Loading:
470: 			data.PlanRunningCount++
471: 		case inst.Status == session.Paused:
472: 			data.PlanPausedCount++
473: 		default:
474: 			data.PlanReadyCount++
475: 		}
476: 	}
477: 	// Include wave progress if an orchestrator exists for this plan.
478: 	if orch, ok := m.waveOrchestrators[planFile]; ok {
479: 		data.TotalWaves = orch.TotalWaves()
480: 		data.TotalTasks = orch.TotalTasks()
481: 		tasks := orch.CurrentWaveTasks()
482: 		data.WaveTasks = make([]ui.WaveTaskInfo, len(tasks))
483: 		for i, task := range tasks {
484: 			state := "pending"
485: 			if orch.IsTaskComplete(task.Number) {
486: 				state = "complete"
487: 			} else if orch.IsTaskFailed(task.Number) {
488: 				state = "failed"
489: 			} else if orch.IsTaskRunning(task.Number) {
490: 				state = "running"
491: 			}
492: 			data.WaveTasks[i] = ui.WaveTaskInfo{Number: task.Number, State: state}
493: 		}
494: 	}
495: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetInfoData(data)
496: }
497: 
498: // updateInfoPane refreshes the info tab data from the selected instance or plan header.
499: func (m *home) updateInfoPane() {
500: 	selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
501: 	if selected == nil {
502: 		// No instance selected — check if a plan header is selected.
503: 		if m.nav.IsSelectedPlanHeader() {
504: 			m.updateInfoPaneForPlanHeader()
505: 			return
506: 		}
507: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetInfoData(ui.InfoData{HasInstance: false})
508: 		return
509: 	}
510: 
511: 	data := ui.InfoData{
512: 		HasInstance: true,
513: 		Title:       selected.Title,
514: 		Program:     selected.Program,
515: 		Branch:      selected.Branch,
516: 		Path:        selected.Path,
517: 		Status:      statusString(selected.Status),
518: 		AgentType:   selected.AgentType,
519: 		TaskNumber:  selected.TaskNumber,
520: 		WaveNumber:  selected.WaveNumber,
521: 	}
522: 
523: 	if !selected.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
524: 		data.Created = selected.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04")
525: 	}
526: 
527: 	if selected.TaskFile != "" {
528: 		if m.taskState != nil {
529: 			entry, ok := m.taskState.Entry(selected.TaskFile)
530: 			if ok {
531: 				data.HasPlan = true
532: 				data.PlanName = taskstate.DisplayName(selected.TaskFile)
533: 				data.PlanDescription = entry.Description
534: 				data.PlanStatus = string(entry.Status)
535: 				data.PlanTopic = entry.Topic
536: 				data.PlanBranch = entry.Branch
537: 				if !entry.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
538: 					data.PlanCreated = entry.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02")
539: 				}
540: 			}
541: 		}
542: 
543: 		if orch, ok := m.waveOrchestrators[selected.TaskFile]; ok {
544: 			data.TotalWaves = orch.TotalWaves()
545: 			data.TotalTasks = orch.TotalTasks()
546: 			tasks := orch.CurrentWaveTasks()
547: 			data.WaveTasks = make([]ui.WaveTaskInfo, len(tasks))
548: 			for i, task := range tasks {
549: 				state := "pending"
550: 				if orch.IsTaskComplete(task.Number) {
551: 					state = "complete"
552: 				} else if orch.IsTaskFailed(task.Number) {
553: 					state = "failed"
554: 				} else if orch.IsTaskRunning(task.Number) {
555: 					state = "running"
556: 				}
557: 				data.WaveTasks[i] = ui.WaveTaskInfo{Number: task.Number, State: state}
558: 			}
559: 		}
560: 	}
561: 
562: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetInfoData(data)
563: }

565: // loadTaskState reads plan state from the store for the active repo.
566: // Called on user-triggered events (plan creation, repo switch, etc.). The periodic
567: // metadata tick loads plan state in its goroutine instead.
568: // Silently no-ops if the store is not configured.
569: func (m *home) loadTaskState() {
570: 	if m.taskStateDir == "" || m.taskStore == nil {
571: 		return
572: 	}
573: 	ps, err := taskstate.Load(m.taskStore, m.taskStoreProject, m.taskStateDir)
574: 	if err != nil {
575: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not load plan state: %v", err)
576: 		if m.toastManager != nil {
577: 			m.toastManager.Error("plan store error: " + err.Error())
578: 		}
579: 		return
580: 	}
581: 	m.taskState = ps
582: }
583: 
584: // updateSidebarTasks pushes the current plans into the sidebar using the three-level tree API.
585: func (m *home) updateSidebarTasks() {
586: 	if m.taskState == nil {
587: 		m.nav.SetTopicsAndPlans(nil, nil, nil)
588: 		return
589: 	}
590: 
591: 	// Build topic displays
592: 	topicInfos := m.taskState.Topics()
593: 	topics := make([]ui.TopicDisplay, 0, len(topicInfos))
594: 	for _, t := range topicInfos {
595: 		plans := m.taskState.TasksByTopic(t.Name)
596: 		planDisplays := make([]ui.PlanDisplay, 0, len(plans))
597: 		for _, p := range plans {
598: 			if p.Status == taskstate.StatusDone || p.Status == taskstate.StatusCancelled {
599: 				continue // finished/cancelled plans handled separately
600: 			}
601: 			planDisplays = append(planDisplays, ui.PlanDisplay{
602: 				Filename:    p.Filename,
603: 				Status:      string(p.Status),
604: 				Description: p.Description,
605: 				Branch:      p.Branch,
606: 				Topic:       p.Topic,
607: 			})
608: 		}
609: 		if len(planDisplays) > 0 {
610: 			topics = append(topics, ui.TopicDisplay{Name: t.Name, Plans: planDisplays})
611: 		}
612: 	}
613: 
614: 	// Build ungrouped plans
615: 	ungroupedInfos := m.taskState.UngroupedTasks()
616: 	ungrouped := make([]ui.PlanDisplay, 0, len(ungroupedInfos))
617: 	for _, p := range ungroupedInfos {
618: 		ungrouped = append(ungrouped, ui.PlanDisplay{
619: 			Filename:    p.Filename,
620: 			Status:      string(p.Status),
621: 			Description: p.Description,
622: 			Branch:      p.Branch,
623: 		})
624: 	}
625: 
626: 	// Flatten single-plan topics where topic name matches the plan display name.
627: 	// These don't benefit from a topic header — show the plan directly as ungrouped.
628: 	filtered := topics[:0]
629: 	for _, t := range topics {
630: 		if len(t.Plans) == 1 && t.Name == taskstate.DisplayName(t.Plans[0].Filename) {
631: 			ungrouped = append(ungrouped, t.Plans[0])
632: 		} else {
633: 			filtered = append(filtered, t)
634: 		}
635: 	}
636: 	topics = filtered
637: 
638: 	// Build history
639: 	finishedInfos := m.taskState.Finished()
640: 	history := make([]ui.PlanDisplay, 0, len(finishedInfos))
641: 	for _, p := range finishedInfos {
642: 		history = append(history, ui.PlanDisplay{
643: 			Filename:    p.Filename,
644: 			Status:      string(p.Status),
645: 			Description: p.Description,
646: 			Branch:      p.Branch,
647: 			Topic:       p.Topic,
648: 		})
649: 	}
650: 
651: 	// Set plan statuses before the rebuild so navPlanSortKey uses
652: 	// up-to-date running/notification flags in a single pass.
653: 	m.nav.SetPlanStatuses(m.computePlanStatuses())
654: 
655: 	m.nav.SetTopicsAndPlans(topics, ungrouped, history)
656: 
657: }
658: 
659: // checkPlanCompletion scans running coder instances for plans that have been
660: // marked "done" by the agent and, if found, transitions them to reviewer sessions.
661: // Returns a cmd to start the reviewer (may be nil).
662: func (m *home) checkPlanCompletion() tea.Cmd {
663: 	if m.taskState == nil {
664: 		return nil
665: 	}
666: 	// Guard: if a reviewer already exists for a plan, do not spawn another.
667: 	// The async metadata tick can overwrite m.taskState with a stale snapshot
668: 	// that still shows StatusDone after transitionToReview already ran and set
669: 	// StatusReviewing. Without this guard, a second reviewer is spawned.
670: 	reviewerPlans := make(map[string]bool)
671: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
672: 		if inst.IsReviewer && inst.TaskFile != "" {
673: 			reviewerPlans[inst.TaskFile] = true
674: 		}
675: 	}
676: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
677: 		if inst.TaskFile == "" || inst.IsReviewer {
678: 			continue
679: 		}
680: 		if inst.ImplementationComplete {
681: 			continue // already went through review cycle — don't re-trigger
682: 		}
683: 		if reviewerPlans[inst.TaskFile] {
684: 			continue // reviewer already spawned; skip regardless of stale plan state
685: 		}
686: 		if !m.taskState.IsDone(inst.TaskFile) {
687: 			continue
688: 		}
689: 		return m.transitionToReview(inst)
690: 	}
691: 	return nil
692: }
693: 
694: // transitionToReview marks a plan as "reviewing", pauses the coder session,
695: // spawns a reviewer session with the reviewer profile, and returns the start cmd.
696: func (m *home) transitionToReview(coderInst *session.Instance) tea.Cmd {
697: 	// Guard: transition via FSM before next tick re-reads disk, preventing double-spawn.
698: 	planFile := coderInst.TaskFile
699: 	if err := m.fsm.Transition(planFile, taskfsm.ImplementFinished); err != nil {
700: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not set plan %q to reviewing: %v", planFile, err)
701: 		// Mark complete to break the retry loop — checkPlanCompletion fires
702: 		// every tick and would re-attempt this transition indefinitely.
703: 		coderInst.ImplementationComplete = true
704: 		return nil // FSM rejected — plan is not in implementing state, don't spawn reviewer.
705: 	}
706: 
707: 	// Auto-pause the coder instance — its work is done.
708: 	coderInst.ImplementationComplete = true
709: 	if err := coderInst.Pause(); err != nil {
710: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not pause coder instance for %q: %v", planFile, err)
711: 	}
712: 
713: 	return m.spawnReviewer(planFile)
714: }
715: 
716: // spawnReviewer creates and starts a reviewer session for the given plan,
717: // using the plan's shared worktree so it reviews the actual implementation branch.
718: // Does NOT perform any FSM transition — the caller is responsible for that.
719: // Solo agent plans are excluded — the user ends those manually.
720: func (m *home) spawnReviewer(planFile string) tea.Cmd {
721: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
722: 		if inst.TaskFile == planFile && inst.SoloAgent {
723: 			return nil
724: 		}
725: 	}
726: 	planName := taskstate.DisplayName(planFile)
727: 	planPath := "docs/plans/" + planFile
728: 	prompt := scaffold.LoadReviewPrompt(planPath, planName)
729: 
730: 	// Kill any previous reviewer for this plan so the new session gets a fresh
731: 	// tmux session instead of reattaching to a stale/errored one.
732: 	m.killExistingPlanAgent(planFile, session.AgentTypeReviewer)
733: 
734: 	// Resolve the plan's branch for the shared worktree.
735: 	branch := m.taskBranch(planFile)
736: 	if branch == "" {
737: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not resolve branch for plan %q", planFile)
738: 		return nil
739: 	}
740: 
741: 	cycle, _ := m.taskState.ReviewCycle(planFile)
742: 	reviewerInst, err := session.NewInstance(session.InstanceOptions{
743: 		Title:       fmt.Sprintf("%s-review-%d", planName, cycle+1),
744: 		Path:        m.activeRepoPath,
745: 		Program:     m.programForAgent(session.AgentTypeReviewer),
746: 		TaskFile:    planFile,
747: 		AgentType:   session.AgentTypeReviewer,
748: 		ReviewCycle: cycle + 1,
749: 	})
750: 	if err != nil {
751: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not create reviewer instance for %q: %v", planFile, err)
752: 		return nil
753: 	}
754: 	reviewerInst.IsReviewer = true
755: 	reviewerInst.QueuedPrompt = prompt
756: 	reviewerInst.SetStatus(session.Loading)
757: 
758: 	m.addInstanceFinalizer(reviewerInst, m.nav.AddInstance(reviewerInst))
759: 	m.nav.SelectInstance(reviewerInst) // sort-order safe, unlike index arithmetic
760: 
761: 	m.audit(auditlog.EventAgentSpawned, fmt.Sprintf("spawned reviewer for %s", planName),
762: 		auditlog.WithPlan(planFile),
763: 		auditlog.WithInstance(reviewerInst.Title),
764: 		auditlog.WithAgent(session.AgentTypeReviewer),
765: 	)
766: 
767: 	m.toastManager.Success(fmt.Sprintf("implementation complete → review started for %s", planName))
768: 
769: 	shared := gitpkg.NewSharedTaskWorktree(m.activeRepoPath, branch)
770: 	return func() tea.Msg {
771: 		if err := shared.Setup(); err != nil {
772: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: reviewerInst, err: err}
773: 		}
774: 		if err := m.materializeTaskFile(planFile, shared.GetWorktreePath()); err != nil {
775: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: reviewerInst, err: err}
776: 		}
777: 		err := reviewerInst.StartInSharedWorktree(shared, branch)
778: 		return instanceStartedMsg{instance: reviewerInst, err: err}
779: 	}
780: }

782: func withOpenCodeModelFlag(program, model string) string {
783: 	model = normalizeOpenCodeModelID(model)
784: 	if model == "" {
785: 		return program
786: 	}
787: 
788: 	tokens := strings.Fields(program)
789: 	if len(tokens) == 0 {
790: 		return program
791: 	}
792: 	if filepath.Base(tokens[0]) != "opencode" {
793: 		return program
794: 	}
795: 
796: 	for i, tok := range tokens {
797: 		if tok == "--model" || tok == "-m" {
798: 			if i+1 < len(tokens) {
799: 				return program
800: 			}
801: 			return program
802: 		}
803: 		if strings.HasPrefix(tok, "--model=") {
804: 			return program
805: 		}
806: 	}
807: 
808: 	return program + " --model " + model
809: }
810: 
811: // programForAgent resolves the program command for a given agent type
812: // (e.g. "coder", "planner") using the kasmos config profile. Falls back to
813: // m.program if no profile is configured.
814: //
815: // For typed agents (coder/planner/reviewer), opencode's own --agent flag
816: // handles model selection via its agent config, so we do NOT append --model.
817: // For ad-hoc instances (no agent type), we append --model since there is no
818: // --agent flag to drive model selection.
819: func (m *home) programForAgent(agentType string) string {
820: 	if m.appConfig == nil {
821: 		return m.program
822: 	}
823: 	// Map agent types to config phases or profile names.
824: 	var profile config.AgentProfile
825: 	switch agentType {
826: 	case session.AgentTypeCoder:
827: 		profile = m.appConfig.ResolveProfile("implementing", m.program)
828: 	case session.AgentTypePlanner:
829: 		profile = m.appConfig.ResolveProfile("planning", m.program)
830: 	case session.AgentTypeReviewer:
831: 		profile = m.appConfig.ResolveProfile("quality_review", m.program)
832: 	case session.AgentTypeFixer:
833: 		profile = m.appConfig.ResolveProfile("fixer", m.program)
834: 	default:
835: 		// Ad-hoc — use the "chat" profile if available.
836: 		if p, ok := m.appConfig.Profiles["chat"]; ok && p.Enabled && p.Program != "" {
837: 			profile = p
838: 		} else {
839: 			return m.program
840: 		}
841: 		// Ad-hoc sessions have no --agent flag, so pass --model explicitly.
842: 		prog := profile.BuildCommand()
843: 		return withOpenCodeModelFlag(prog, profile.Model)
844: 	}
845: 	// Typed agents: opencode handles model via --agent <type> + its own config.
846: 	return profile.BuildCommand()
847: }
848: 
849: func normalizeOpenCodeModelID(model string) string {
850: 	model = strings.TrimSpace(model)
851: 	if model == "" || strings.Contains(model, "/") {
852: 		return model
853: 	}
854: 	if strings.HasPrefix(model, "claude-") {
855: 		return "anthropic/" + model
856: 	}
857: 	return model
858: }

860: // killExistingPlanAgent finds and kills any existing instance for the given plan
861: // and agent type, removing it from both the UI list and persistence list.
862: //
863: // IMPORTANT: Instances are removed from both lists BEFORE killing the tmux
864: // session. This prevents the metadata-tick death-detection from seeing a dead
865: // reviewer in the list and auto-firing ReviewApproved (which would prematurely
866: // mark the plan as done).
867: //
868: // For reviewers, also matches legacy instances that only have IsReviewer set.
869: func (m *home) killExistingPlanAgent(planFile, agentType string) {
870: 	// First pass: identify matching instances by title.
871: 	var titles []string
872: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
873: 		if inst.TaskFile != planFile {
874: 			continue
875: 		}
876: 		match := inst.AgentType == agentType
877: 		// Legacy reviewer instances may only have IsReviewer without AgentType.
878: 		if !match && agentType == session.AgentTypeReviewer && inst.IsReviewer {
879: 			match = true
880: 		}
881: 		if match {
882: 			titles = append(titles, inst.Title)
883: 		}
884: 	}
885: 
886: 	// Second pass: remove from both lists, then kill tmux.
887: 	// Removal first ensures the death-detection tick cannot see the dead instance.
888: 	for _, title := range titles {
889: 		inst := m.nav.RemoveByTitle(title)
890: 		m.removeFromAllInstances(title)
891: 		// Invalidate the preview terminal cache so that a replacement instance
892: 		// with the same title (e.g. a new "applying fixes" coder) gets a fresh
893: 		// terminal instead of showing stale output from the killed session.
894: 		if title == m.previewTerminalInstance {
895: 			if m.previewTerminal != nil {
896: 				m.previewTerminal.Close()
897: 			}
898: 			m.previewTerminal = nil
899: 			m.previewTerminalInstance = ""
900: 		}
901: 		if inst != nil {
902: 			if err := inst.Kill(); err != nil {
903: 				log.WarningLog.Printf("could not kill old %s for %q: %v", agentType, planFile, err)
904: 			}
905: 		}
906: 	}
907: }
908: 
909: // spawnCoderWithFeedback creates and starts a coder session for the given plan,
910: // injecting reviewer feedback into the implementation prompt. Uses the plan's
911: // shared worktree so fixes are applied to the actual implementation branch.
912: // Does NOT perform any FSM transition — the caller is responsible for that.
913: func (m *home) spawnCoderWithFeedback(planFile, feedback string) tea.Cmd {
914: 	planName := taskstate.DisplayName(planFile)
915: 	prompt := buildImplementPrompt(planFile)
916: 	if feedback != "" {
917: 		prompt += fmt.Sprintf("\n\nReviewer feedback from previous round:\n%s", feedback)
918: 	}
919: 
920: 	// Kill any previous coder for this plan so the new session gets a fresh
921: 	// tmux session instead of reattaching to a stale/errored one.
922: 	m.killExistingPlanAgent(planFile, session.AgentTypeCoder)
923: 
924: 	// Clear the push-prompt dedup flag so this new coder round can trigger
925: 	// the push dialog when it finishes.
926: 	delete(m.coderPushPrompted, planFile)
927: 
928: 	// Resolve the plan's branch for the shared worktree.
929: 	branch := m.taskBranch(planFile)
930: 	if branch == "" {
931: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not resolve branch for plan %q", planFile)
932: 		return nil
933: 	}
934: 
935: 	cycle, _ := m.taskState.ReviewCycle(planFile)
936: 	coderInst, err := session.NewInstance(session.InstanceOptions{
937: 		Title:       fmt.Sprintf("%s-fix-%d", planName, cycle),
938: 		Path:        m.activeRepoPath,
939: 		Program:     m.programForAgent(session.AgentTypeCoder),
940: 		TaskFile:    planFile,
941: 		AgentType:   session.AgentTypeCoder,
942: 		ReviewCycle: cycle,
943: 	})
944: 	if err != nil {
945: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not create coder instance for %q: %v", planFile, err)
946: 		return nil
947: 	}
948: 	coderInst.QueuedPrompt = prompt
949: 	coderInst.SetStatus(session.Loading)
950: 
951: 	m.addInstanceFinalizer(coderInst, m.nav.AddInstance(coderInst))
952: 	m.nav.SelectInstance(coderInst)
953: 
954: 	detail := ""
955: 	if feedback != "" {
956: 		if len(feedback) > 200 {
957: 			detail = feedback[:200] + "..."
958: 		} else {
959: 			detail = feedback
960: 		}
961: 	}
962: 	m.audit(auditlog.EventAgentSpawned, fmt.Sprintf("spawned coder with reviewer feedback for %s", planName),
963: 		auditlog.WithPlan(planFile),
964: 		auditlog.WithInstance(coderInst.Title),
965: 		auditlog.WithAgent(session.AgentTypeCoder),
966: 		auditlog.WithDetail(detail),
967: 	)
968: 
969: 	m.toastManager.Info(fmt.Sprintf("review changes requested → re-implementing %s", planName))
970: 
971: 	shared := gitpkg.NewSharedTaskWorktree(m.activeRepoPath, branch)
972: 	return func() tea.Msg {
973: 		if err := shared.Setup(); err != nil {
974: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: coderInst, err: err}
975: 		}
976: 		if err := m.materializeTaskFile(planFile, shared.GetWorktreePath()); err != nil {
977: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: coderInst, err: err}
978: 		}
979: 		err := coderInst.StartInSharedWorktree(shared, branch)
980: 		return instanceStartedMsg{instance: coderInst, err: err}
981: 	}
982: }

984: func (m *home) materializeTaskFile(planFile, repoPath string) error {
985: 	if m.taskStore == nil {
986: 		return nil
987: 	}
988: 	content, err := m.taskStore.GetContent(m.taskStoreProject, planFile)
989: 	if err != nil {
990: 		return fmt.Errorf("get plan content %s: %w", planFile, err)
991: 	}
992: 	plansDir := filepath.Join(repoPath, "docs", "plans")
993: 	if err := os.MkdirAll(plansDir, 0o755); err != nil {
994: 		return fmt.Errorf("create plans dir: %w", err)
995: 	}
996: 	planPath := filepath.Join(plansDir, planFile)
997: 	if err := os.WriteFile(planPath, []byte(content), 0o644); err != nil {
998: 		return fmt.Errorf("write plan file: %w", err)
999: 	}
1000: 	return nil
1001: }

1003: // ingestTaskContent reads the plan markdown file from the given repo path and
1004: // stores its content in the DB via the plan store. This bridges the gap between
1005: // agents writing plan files to their worktree and the DB being the source of truth.
1006: // Called when a PlannerFinished signal is processed.
1007: func (m *home) ingestTaskContent(planFile, repoPath string) {
1008: 	if m.taskStore == nil || m.taskState == nil {
1009: 		return
1010: 	}
1011: 	planPath := filepath.Join(repoPath, "docs", "plans", planFile)
1012: 	data, err := os.ReadFile(planPath)
1013: 	if err != nil {
1014: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("ingestTaskContent: cannot read %s: %v", planPath, err)
1015: 		return
1016: 	}
1017: 	if err := m.taskState.SetContent(planFile, string(data)); err != nil {
1018: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("ingestTaskContent: cannot store content for %s: %v", planFile, err)
1019: 	}
1020: }

1022: // viewSelectedPlan renders the selected plan's markdown in the preview pane.
1023: // The rendered output is cached; on cache miss the glamour render runs async
1024: // via a tea.Cmd so the UI stays responsive.
1025: func (m *home) viewSelectedPlan() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1026: 	planFile := m.nav.GetSelectedPlanFile()
1027: 	if planFile == "" {
1028: 		return m, nil
1029: 	}
1030: 
1031: 	// Cache hit — reuse previously rendered content (instant).
1032: 	if planFile == m.cachedPlanFile && m.cachedPlanRendered != "" {
1033: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.PreviewTab)
1034: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetDocumentContent(m.cachedPlanRendered)
1035: 		return m, nil
1036: 	}
1037: 
1038: 	// Cache miss — render async so the UI doesn't freeze.
1039: 	previewWidth, _ := m.tabbedWindow.GetPreviewSize()
1040: 	wordWrap := previewWidth - 4
1041: 	if wordWrap < 40 {
1042: 		wordWrap = 40
1043: 	}
1044: 
1045: 	return m, func() tea.Msg {
1046: 		data, err := m.taskStore.GetContent(m.taskStoreProject, planFile)
1047: 		if err != nil {
1048: 			return planRenderedMsg{err: fmt.Errorf("could not read plan %s: %w", planFile, err)}
1049: 		}
1050: 
1051: 		renderer, err := glamour.NewTermRenderer(
1052: 			glamour.WithStandardStyle("dark"),
1053: 			glamour.WithWordWrap(wordWrap),
1054: 		)
1055: 		if err != nil {
1056: 			return planRenderedMsg{err: fmt.Errorf("could not create markdown renderer: %w", err)}
1057: 		}
1058: 
1059: 		rendered, err := renderer.Render(data)
1060: 		if err != nil {
1061: 			return planRenderedMsg{err: fmt.Errorf("could not render markdown: %w", err)}
1062: 		}
1063: 
1064: 		return planRenderedMsg{planFile: planFile, rendered: rendered}
1065: 	}
1066: }

1068: // createTaskEntry creates a new plan entry in the store.
1069: func (m *home) createTaskEntry(name, description, topic string) error {
1070: 	if m.taskState == nil {
1071: 		if m.taskStore == nil {
1072: 			return fmt.Errorf("plan store not configured")
1073: 		}
1074: 		ps, err := taskstate.Load(m.taskStore, m.taskStoreProject, m.taskStateDir)
1075: 		if err != nil {
1076: 			return err
1077: 		}
1078: 		m.taskState = ps
1079: 	}
1080: 
1081: 	slug := slugifyPlanName(name)
1082: 	filename := slug + ".md"
1083: 	branch := "plan/" + slug
1084: 	if err := m.taskState.Create(filename, description, branch, topic, time.Now().UTC()); err != nil {
1085: 		if m.toastManager != nil {
1086: 			m.toastManager.Error("plan store error: " + err.Error())
1087: 		}
1088: 		return err
1089: 	}
1090: 	m.audit(auditlog.EventPlanCreated, "created plan", auditlog.WithPlan(filename))
1091: 	m.updateSidebarTasks()
1092: 	return nil
1093: }

1095: // slugifyPlanName converts a plan name to a URL-safe slug.
1096: func slugifyPlanName(name string) string {
1097: 	name = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(name))
1098: 	name = regexp.MustCompile(`[^a-z0-9]+`).ReplaceAllString(name, "-")
1099: 	return strings.Trim(name, "-")
1100: }

1102: // buildPlanFilename derives the plan filename from a human name.
1103: // "Auth Refactor" → "auth-refactor.md"
1104: func buildPlanFilename(name string, _ time.Time) string {
1105: 	slug := slugifyPlanName(name)
1106: 	if slug == "" {
1107: 		slug = "plan"
1108: 	}
1109: 	return slug + ".md"
1110: }

1114: 	return fmt.Sprintf("# %s\n\n## Context\n\n%s\n\n## Notes\n\n- Created by kas lifecycle flow\n- Plan file: %s\n", name, description, filename)

1117: // createPlanRecord registers the plan in the store.

1119: 	if m.taskState == nil {
1120: 		if m.taskStore == nil {
1121: 			return fmt.Errorf("plan store not configured")
1122: 		}
1123: 		ps, err := taskstate.Load(m.taskStore, m.taskStoreProject, m.taskStateDir)
1124: 		if err != nil {
1125: 			return err
1126: 		}
1127: 		m.taskState = ps
1128: 	}
1129: 	if err := m.taskState.Register(planFile, description, branch, now); err != nil {
1130: 		if m.toastManager != nil {

1133: 		return err
1134: 	}
1135: 	return nil

1138: // finalizePlanCreation writes the plan stub content to the store, registers it,
1139: // and creates the feature branch. Called at the end of the plan creation wizard.
1140: func (m *home) finalizePlanCreation(name, description string) error {
1141: 	now := time.Now().UTC()
1142: 	planFile := buildPlanFilename(name, now)
1143: 	branch := gitpkg.TaskBranchFromFile(planFile)
1144: 	content := renderPlanStub(name, description, planFile)
1145: 	if err := m.createPlanRecord(planFile, description, branch, now); err != nil {
1146: 		return err
1147: 	}
1148: 	if err := m.taskState.SetContent(planFile, content); err != nil {
1149: 		return err
1150: 	}
1151: 	if err := gitpkg.EnsureTaskBranch(m.activeRepoPath, branch); err != nil {
1152: 		return err
1153: 	}
1154: 
1155: 	m.loadTaskState()
1156: 	m.updateSidebarTasks()
1157: 	return nil
1158: }

1160: func (m *home) importClickUpTask(task *clickup.Task) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1161: 	if task == nil {
1162: 		m.toastManager.Error("clickup fetch failed: empty task payload")
1163: 		return m, m.toastTickCmd()
1164: 	}
1165: 
1166: 	filename := clickup.ScaffoldFilename(task.Name)
1167: 
1168: 	if m.taskState == nil {
1169: 		m.loadTaskState()
1170: 	}
1171: 	if m.taskState == nil {
1172: 		m.toastManager.Error("failed to register imported plan: plan state unavailable")
1173: 		return m, m.toastTickCmd()
1174: 	}
1175: 
1176: 	filename = dedupePlanFilenameInState(m.taskState, filename)
1177: 
1178: 	scaffold := clickup.ScaffoldPlan(*task)
1179: 
1180: 	branch := gitpkg.TaskBranchFromFile(filename)
1181: 	if err := m.taskState.Register(filename, task.Name, branch, time.Now()); err != nil {
1182: 		m.toastManager.Error("failed to register imported plan: " + err.Error())
1183: 		return m, m.toastTickCmd()
1184: 	}
1185: 	if err := m.taskState.SetContent(filename, scaffold); err != nil {
1186: 		m.toastManager.Error("failed to save imported plan content: " + err.Error())
1187: 		return m, m.toastTickCmd()
1188: 	}
1189: 	if task.ID != "" {
1190: 		if err := m.taskState.SetClickUpTaskID(filename, task.ID); err != nil {
1191: 			log.WarningLog.Printf("importClickUpTask: failed to set clickup task id for %q: %v", filename, err)
1192: 		}
1193: 	}
1194: 
1195: 	if err := m.fsm.Transition(filename, taskfsm.PlanStart); err != nil {
1196: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("clickup import transition failed for %q: %v", filename, err)
1197: 	}
1198: 
1199: 	m.loadTaskState()
1200: 	m.updateSidebarTasks()
1201: 
1202: 	prompt := fmt.Sprintf(`Analyze this imported ClickUp task. The task details and subtasks are included as reference in the plan file.
1203: 
1204: Determine if the task is well-specified enough for implementation or needs further analysis. Write a proper implementation plan with ## Wave sections, task breakdowns, architecture notes, and tech stack. Use the ClickUp subtasks as a starting point but reorganize into waves based on dependencies.
1205: 
1206: The plan file is at: docs/plans/%s`, filename)
1207: 
1208: 	m.toastManager.Success("imported! spawning planner...")
1209: 	model, cmd := m.spawnTaskAgent(filename, "plan", prompt)
1210: 	if cmd == nil {
1211: 		return model, m.toastTickCmd()
1212: 	}
1213: 	return model, tea.Batch(cmd, m.toastTickCmd())
1214: }
1215: 
1216: func dedupePlanFilename(plansDir, filename string) string {
1217: 	planPath := filepath.Join(plansDir, filename)
1218: 	if _, err := os.Stat(planPath); os.IsNotExist(err) {
1219: 		return filename
1220: 	}
1221: 
1222: 	base := strings.TrimSuffix(filename, ".md")
1223: 	for i := 2; i < 100; i++ {
1224: 		candidate := fmt.Sprintf("%s-%d.md", base, i)
1225: 		if _, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(plansDir, candidate)); os.IsNotExist(err) {
1226: 			return candidate
1227: 		}
1228: 	}
1229: 
1230: 	return filename
1231: }
1232: 
1233: func dedupePlanFilenameInState(ps *taskstate.TaskState, filename string) string {
1234: 	if ps == nil {
1235: 		return filename
1236: 	}
1237: 	if _, ok := ps.Entry(filename); !ok {
1238: 		return filename
1239: 	}
1240: 
1241: 	base := strings.TrimSuffix(filename, ".md")
1242: 	for i := 2; i < 100; i++ {
1243: 		candidate := fmt.Sprintf("%s-%d.md", base, i)
1244: 		if _, ok := ps.Entry(candidate); !ok {
1245: 			return candidate
1246: 		}
1247: 	}
1248: 
1249: 	return filename
1250: }
1251: 
1252: // shouldPromptPushAfterCoderExit returns true when a coder session has finished
1253: // and the plan is still in the implementing state. A coder is considered finished
1254: // when its tmux session has exited (tmuxAlive == false) OR when it has returned
1255: // to a prompt after completing its queued work (PromptDetected && !AwaitingWork).
1256: // The latter covers "applying fixes" coders spawned by spawnCoderWithFeedback,
1257: // which finish their work and return to prompt rather than exiting tmux.
1258: func shouldPromptPushAfterCoderExit(entry taskstate.TaskEntry, inst *session.Instance, tmuxAlive bool) bool {
1259: 	if inst == nil {
1260: 		return false
1261: 	}
1262: 	if inst.TaskFile == "" {
1263: 		return false
1264: 	}
1265: 	if inst.AgentType != session.AgentTypeCoder {
1266: 		return false
1267: 	}
1268: 	if inst.SoloAgent {
1269: 		return false
1270: 	}
1271: 	if entry.Status != taskstate.StatusImplementing {
1272: 		return false
1273: 	}
1274: 	// Tmux exited — original single-coder completion path.
1275: 	if !tmuxAlive {
1276: 		return true
1277: 	}
1278: 	// Agent returned to prompt after finishing queued work — covers the
1279: 	// review-feedback coder ("applying fixes") which stays alive in tmux.
1280: 	if inst.PromptDetected && !inst.AwaitingWork {
1281: 		return true
1282: 	}
1283: 	return false
1284: }
1285: 
1286: // promptPushBranchThenAdvance shows a confirmation overlay asking the user to
1287: // push the implementation branch, then advances the plan to reviewing and
1288: // spawns a reviewer agent via coderCompleteMsg.
1289: func (m *home) promptPushBranchThenAdvance(inst *session.Instance) tea.Cmd {
1290: 	capturedPlanFile := inst.TaskFile
1291: 	// Mark as prompted so the metadata tick doesn't re-trigger the dialog
1292: 	// while the user is deciding or after they dismiss it.
1293: 	if m.coderPushPrompted == nil {
1294: 		m.coderPushPrompted = make(map[string]bool)
1295: 	}
1296: 	m.coderPushPrompted[capturedPlanFile] = true
1297: 	message := fmt.Sprintf("[!] implementation finished for '%s'. push branch now?", taskstate.DisplayName(capturedPlanFile))
1298: 	pushAction := func() tea.Msg {
1299: 		worktree, err := inst.GetGitWorktree()
1300: 		if err == nil {
1301: 			_ = worktree.Push(false)
1302: 		}
1303: 		return coderCompleteMsg{planFile: capturedPlanFile}
1304: 	}
1305: 	return m.confirmAction(message, func() tea.Msg { return pushAction() })
1306: }
1307: 
1308: // taskBranch resolves the branch name for a plan, backfilling if needed.
1309: func (m *home) taskBranch(planFile string) string {
1310: 	if m.taskState == nil {
1311: 		return ""
1312: 	}
1313: 	entry, ok := m.taskState.Entry(planFile)
1314: 	if !ok {
1315: 		return ""
1316: 	}
1317: 	if entry.Branch == "" {
1318: 		entry.Branch = gitpkg.TaskBranchFromFile(planFile)
1319: 		_ = m.taskState.SetBranch(planFile, entry.Branch)
1320: 	}
1321: 	return entry.Branch
1322: }
1323: 
1324: // buildPlanningPrompt returns the initial prompt for a planner agent session.
1325: // The prompt explicitly requires ## Wave N headers because kasmos uses them
1326: // for wave orchestration — without them, implementation cannot start.
1327: func buildPlanningPrompt(planName, description string) string {
1328: 	return fmt.Sprintf(
1329: 		"Plan %s. Goal: %s. "+
1330: 			"Use the `kasmos-planner` skill. "+
1331: 			"The plan MUST include ## Wave N sections (at minimum ## Wave 1) "+
1332: 			"grouping all tasks — kasmos requires Wave headers to orchestrate implementation.",
1333: 		planName, description,
1334: 	)
1335: }
1336: 
1337: // buildWaveAnnotationPrompt returns the prompt used when a planner is respawned
1338: // to add ## Wave headers to an existing plan that is missing them.
1339: // It instructs the planner to annotate the plan, commit the change, and write
1340: // the sentinel signal so kasmos can resume the implementation flow.
1341: func buildWaveAnnotationPrompt(planFile string) string {
1342: 	return fmt.Sprintf(
1343: 		"The plan at docs/plans/%[1]s is missing ## Wave N headers required for kasmos wave orchestration. "+
1344: 			"Please annotate the plan by wrapping all tasks under ## Wave N sections. "+
1345: 			"Every plan needs at least ## Wave 1 — even single-task trivial plans. "+
1346: 			"Keep all existing task content intact; only add the ## Wave headers.\n\n"+
1347: 			"After annotating:\n"+
1348: 			"1. Commit: git add docs/plans/%[1]s && git commit -m \"plan: add wave headers to %[1]s\"\n"+
1349: 			"2. Signal completion: touch .kasmos/signals/planner-finished-%[1]s\n"+
1350: 			"Do not edit plan-state.json directly.",
1351: 		planFile,
1352: 	)
1353: }
1354: 
1355: // buildImplementPrompt returns the prompt for a coder agent session.
1356: // The plan file is materialized to docs/plans/<planFile> in the agent's worktree
1357: // by the TUI before the agent starts. Agents write sentinel signals to
1358: // .kasmos/signals/ in their worktree; the TUI ingests them on completion.
1359: func buildImplementPrompt(planFile string) string {
1360: 	return fmt.Sprintf(
1361: 		"Implement docs/plans/%s using the `kasmos-coder` skill. Execute all tasks sequentially.",
1362: 		planFile,
1363: 	)
1364: }
1365: 
1366: // buildSoloPrompt returns a minimal prompt for a solo agent session.
1367: // If planFile is non-empty, it references the plan file. Otherwise just name + description.
1368: func buildSoloPrompt(planName, description, planFile string) string {
1369: 	if planFile != "" {
1370: 		return fmt.Sprintf("Implement %s. Goal: %s. Plan: docs/plans/%s", planName, description, planFile)
1371: 	}
1372: 	return fmt.Sprintf("Implement %s. Goal: %s.", planName, description)
1373: }
1374: 
1375: // buildModifyTaskPrompt returns the prompt for modifying an existing plan.
1376: func buildModifyTaskPrompt(planFile string) string {
1377: 	return fmt.Sprintf("Modify existing plan at docs/plans/%s. Keep the same filename and update only what changed.", planFile)
1378: }
1379: 
1380: // agentTypeForSubItem maps a sidebar stage name to the corresponding AgentType constant.
1381: func agentTypeForSubItem(action string) (string, bool) {
1382: 	switch action {
1383: 	case "plan":
1384: 		return session.AgentTypePlanner, true
1385: 	case "implement", "solo":
1386: 		return session.AgentTypeCoder, true
1387: 	case "review":
1388: 		return session.AgentTypeReviewer, true
1389: 	default:
1390: 		return "", false
1391: 	}
1392: }
1393: 
1394: // spawnAdHocAgent creates and starts an ad-hoc agent session (no plan, no lifecycle).
1395: // branch and workPath are optional overrides - empty strings use defaults.
1396: func (m *home) spawnAdHocAgent(name, branch, workPath string) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1397: 	path := m.activeRepoPath
1398: 	if workPath != "" {
1399: 		path = workPath
1400: 	}
1401: 
1402: 	inst, err := session.NewInstance(session.InstanceOptions{
1403: 		Title:   name,
1404: 		Path:    path,
1405: 		Program: m.programForAgent(session.AgentTypeFixer),
1406: 	})
1407: 	if err != nil {
1408: 		return m, m.handleError(err)
1409: 	}
1410: 
1411: 	inst.AgentType = session.AgentTypeFixer
1412: 	inst.SetStatus(session.Loading)
1413: 	inst.LoadingTotal = 8
1414: 	inst.LoadingMessage = "preparing session..."
1415: 
1416: 	m.state = stateDefault
1417: 	m.menu.SetState(ui.StateDefault)
1418: 
1419: 	var startCmd tea.Cmd
1420: 	switch {
1421: 	case workPath != "" && branch == "":
1422: 		// Path override only - run in-place on main branch (no worktree)
1423: 		startCmd = func() tea.Msg {
1424: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: inst.StartOnMainBranch()}
1425: 		}
1426: 
1427: 	case branch != "":
1428: 		// Branch override - create worktree on specified branch
1429: 		startCmd = func() tea.Msg {
1430: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: inst.StartOnBranch(branch)}
1431: 		}
1432: 
1433: 	default:
1434: 		// No overrides - run in-place on current branch (no worktree)
1435: 		startCmd = func() tea.Msg {
1436: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: inst.StartOnMainBranch()}
1437: 		}
1438: 	}
1439: 
1440: 	m.audit(auditlog.EventAgentSpawned, fmt.Sprintf("spawned fixer agent: %s", name),
1441: 		auditlog.WithInstance(name),
1442: 		auditlog.WithAgent(session.AgentTypeFixer),
1443: 	)
1444: 
1445: 	m.addInstanceFinalizer(inst, m.nav.AddInstance(inst))
1446: 	m.nav.SelectInstance(inst)
1447: 	return m, tea.Batch(tea.WindowSize(), startCmd)
1448: }
1449: 
1450: // spawnTaskAgent creates and starts an agent session for the given plan and action.
1451: func (m *home) spawnTaskAgent(planFile, action, prompt string) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1452: 	entry, ok := m.taskState.Entry(planFile)
1453: 	if !ok {
1454: 		return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("task not found: %s", planFile))
1455: 	}
1456: 
1457: 	agentType, ok := agentTypeForSubItem(action)
1458: 	if !ok {
1459: 		return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("unknown plan action: %s", action))
1460: 	}
1461: 
1462: 	// Kill any existing instance of the same type for this plan to prevent
1463: 	// duplicates (e.g. user triggers "start review" when a reviewer already exists).
1464: 	if agentType == session.AgentTypeReviewer || agentType == session.AgentTypePlanner {
1465: 		m.killExistingPlanAgent(planFile, agentType)
1466: 	}
1467: 
1468: 	planName := taskstate.DisplayName(planFile)
1469: 	title := planName + "-" + action
1470: 	// reviewCycle is resolved once and reused for both the title and the instance field.
1471: 	var reviewCycle int
1472: 	if action == "solo" {
1473: 		// Solo sessions run on main branch, so their tmux session names must stay
1474: 		// unique to avoid accidentally reattaching to another solo session.
1475: 		title = planName + "-solo"
1476: 	} else if action == "review" {
1477: 		// Use cycle-suffixed title so each review round gets a unique tmux session name.
1478: 		reviewCycle, _ = m.taskState.ReviewCycle(planFile)
1479: 		title = fmt.Sprintf("%s-review-%d", planName, reviewCycle+1)
1480: 	}
1481: 	inst, err := session.NewInstance(session.InstanceOptions{
1482: 		Title:     title,
1483: 		Path:      m.activeRepoPath,
1484: 		Program:   m.programForAgent(agentType),
1485: 		TaskFile:  planFile,
1486: 		AgentType: agentType,
1487: 	})
1488: 	if err != nil {
1489: 		return m, m.handleError(err)
1490: 	}
1491: 	// Keep IsReviewer in sync with AgentType so the reviewer-completion check
1492: 	// in the metadata tick handler (which gates on inst.IsReviewer) fires for
1493: 	// sidebar-spawned reviewers as well as auto-spawned ones.
1494: 	if agentType == session.AgentTypeReviewer {
1495: 		inst.IsReviewer = true
1496: 		// Set ReviewCycle so the instance carries the same cycle number used in the title.
1497: 		inst.ReviewCycle = reviewCycle + 1
1498: 	}
1499: 	if action == "solo" {
1500: 		inst.SoloAgent = true
1501: 	}
1502: 	inst.QueuedPrompt = prompt
1503: 
1504: 	// Set loading state immediately so the UI shows the progress bar
1505: 	// instead of the idle banner while the async start runs.
1506: 	inst.SetStatus(session.Loading)
1507: 	inst.LoadingTotal = 5
1508: 	inst.LoadingMessage = "Preparing session..."
1509: 
1510: 	var startCmd tea.Cmd
1511: 	if action == "plan" || action == "solo" {
1512: 		// Planner and solo agent run on main branch — no worktree created.
1513: 		startCmd = func() tea.Msg {
1514: 			if err := m.materializeTaskFile(planFile, m.activeRepoPath); err != nil {
1515: 				return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: err}
1516: 			}
1517: 			err := inst.StartOnMainBranch()
1518: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: err}
1519: 		}
1520: 	} else {
1521: 		// Backfill branch name for plans created before the branch field was introduced.
1522: 		if entry.Branch == "" {
1523: 			entry.Branch = gitpkg.TaskBranchFromFile(planFile)
1524: 			if err := m.taskState.SetBranch(planFile, entry.Branch); err != nil {
1525: 				return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("failed to assign branch for plan: %w", err))
1526: 			}
1527: 		}
1528: 
1529: 		// Coder and reviewer share the plan's feature branch worktree
1530: 		shared := gitpkg.NewSharedTaskWorktree(m.activeRepoPath, entry.Branch)
1531: 		if err := shared.Setup(); err != nil {
1532: 			return m, m.handleError(err)
1533: 		}
1534: 		startCmd = func() tea.Msg {
1535: 			if err := m.materializeTaskFile(planFile, shared.GetWorktreePath()); err != nil {
1536: 				return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: err}
1537: 			}
1538: 			err := inst.StartInSharedWorktree(shared, entry.Branch)
1539: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: err}
1540: 		}
1541: 	}
1542: 
1543: 	m.audit(auditlog.EventAgentSpawned, fmt.Sprintf("spawned %s for plan %s", agentType, taskstate.DisplayName(planFile)),
1544: 		auditlog.WithPlan(planFile),
1545: 		auditlog.WithInstance(title),

23: func (m *home) handleMenuHighlighting(msg tea.KeyMsg) (cmd tea.Cmd, returnEarly bool) {
24: 	// Handle menu highlighting when you press a button. We intercept it here and immediately return to
25: 	// update the ui while re-sending the keypress. Then, on the next call to this, we actually handle the keypress.
26: 	if m.keySent {
27: 		m.keySent = false
28: 		return nil, false
29: 	}
30: 	if m.state == statePrompt || m.state == stateHelp || m.state == stateConfirm || m.state == stateNewPlan || m.state == stateNewPlanDeriving || m.state == stateNewPlanTopic || m.state == stateSpawnAgent || m.state == stateSearch || m.state == stateContextMenu || m.state == statePRTitle || m.state == statePRBody || m.state == stateRenameInstance || m.state == stateRenameTask || m.state == stateSendPrompt || m.state == stateFocusAgent || m.state == stateChangeTopic || m.state == stateSetStatus || m.state == stateClickUpSearch || m.state == stateClickUpPicker || m.state == stateClickUpFetching || m.state == stateClickUpWorkspacePicker || m.state == statePermission || m.state == stateTmuxBrowser || m.state == stateChatAboutTask {
31: 		return nil, false
32: 	}
33: 	// If it's in the global keymap, we should try to highlight it.
34: 	name, ok := keys.GlobalKeyStringsMap[msg.String()]
35: 	if !ok {
36: 		return nil, false
37: 	}
38: 
39: 	if m.nav.GetSelectedInstance() != nil && m.nav.GetSelectedInstance().Paused() && name == keys.KeyEnter {
40: 		return nil, false
41: 	}
42: 	// (no special-cased keys to skip here)
43: 
44: 	// Skip the menu highlighting if the key is not in the map or we are using the shift up and down keys.
45: 	// TODO: cleanup: when you press enter on stateNew, we use keys.KeySubmitName. We should unify the keymap.
46: 	if name == keys.KeyEnter && m.state == stateNew {
47: 		name = keys.KeySubmitName
48: 	}
49: 	m.keySent = true
50: 	return tea.Batch(
51: 		func() tea.Msg { return msg },
52: 		m.keydownCallback(name)), true
53: }

55: // handleMouse processes mouse events for click and scroll interactions.
56: func (m *home) handleMouse(msg tea.MouseMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
57: 	if msg.Action != tea.MouseActionPress {
58: 		return m, nil
59: 	}
60: 
61: 	// Handle scroll wheel — always scrolls content (never navigates files)
62: 	if msg.Button == tea.MouseButtonWheelUp || msg.Button == tea.MouseButtonWheelDown {
63: 		selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
64: 		if selected != nil && selected.Status != session.Paused {
65: 			switch msg.Button {
66: 			case tea.MouseButtonWheelUp:
67: 				m.tabbedWindow.ContentScrollUp()
68: 			case tea.MouseButtonWheelDown:
69: 				m.tabbedWindow.ContentScrollDown()
70: 			}
71: 		}
72: 		return m, nil
73: 	}
74: 
75: 	// Dismiss overlays on click-outside
76: 	if m.state == stateContextMenu && msg.Button == tea.MouseButtonLeft {
77: 		m.contextMenu = nil
78: 		m.state = stateDefault
79: 		return m, nil
80: 	}

82: 		m.tmuxBrowser = nil
83: 		m.state = stateDefault
84: 		return m, nil
85: 	}
86: 	if m.state != stateDefault {
87: 		return m, nil
88: 	}

90: 	// Right-click: show context menu

92: 		return m.handleRightClick(msg)
93: 	}

95: 	// Only handle left clicks from here

97: 		return m, nil
98: 	}
99: 
100: 	// Zone-based click: search box

106: 	}

108: 	// Zone-based click: tab headers — switch visible tab without stealing sidebar focus.
109: 	for i, zoneID := range ui.TabZoneIDs {

112: 			m.menu.SetInDiffTab(m.tabbedWindow.IsInDiffTab())

114: 		}
115: 	}

125: 		for i := range m.nav.RowCount() {

128: 				m.nav.ClickItem(i)
129: 				return m, m.instanceChanged()
130: 			}
131: 		}
132: 		return m, nil
133: 	}

135: 	// Click in tabbed window area — sidebar retains focus.

191: 			return m, tea.Sequence(
192: 				tea.WindowSize(),
193: 				func() tea.Msg {
194: 					m.menu.SetState(ui.StateDefault)
195: 					return nil
196: 				},
197: 			)
198: 		}
199: 
200: 		instance := m.newInstance
201: 		if instance == nil {
202: 			// stateNew without a pending instance — shouldn't happen, return to default
203: 			m.state = stateDefault
204: 			m.menu.SetState(ui.StateDefault)
205: 			return m, nil
206: 		}
207: 		switch msg.Type {
208: 		// Start the instance (enable previews etc) and go back to the main menu state.
209: 		case tea.KeyEnter:
210: 			if len(instance.Title) == 0 {
211: 				return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("title cannot be empty"))
212: 			}
213: 
214: 			// Set loading status and transition to default state immediately
215: 			instance.SetStatus(session.Loading)
216: 			m.state = stateDefault
217: 			m.newInstance = nil
218: 			m.menu.SetState(ui.StateDefault)
219: 
220: 			// Handle prompt-after-name flow
221: 			if m.promptAfterName {
222: 				m.state = statePrompt
223: 				m.menu.SetState(ui.StatePrompt)
224: 				m.textInputOverlay = overlay.NewTextInputOverlay("enter prompt", "")
225: 				m.textInputOverlay.SetSize(50, 5)
226: 				m.promptAfterName = false
227: 			}
228: 
229: 			// Start instance asynchronously
230: 			startCmd := func() tea.Msg {
231: 				return instanceStartedMsg{instance: instance, err: instance.Start(true)}
232: 			}
233: 
234: 			return m, tea.Batch(tea.WindowSize(), startCmd)
235: 		case tea.KeyRunes:
236: 			if runewidth.StringWidth(instance.Title) >= 32 {
237: 				return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("title cannot be longer than 32 characters"))
238: 			}
239: 			if err := instance.SetTitle(instance.Title + string(msg.Runes)); err != nil {
240: 				return m, m.handleError(err)
241: 			}
242: 		case tea.KeyBackspace:
243: 			runes := []rune(instance.Title)
244: 			if len(runes) == 0 {
245: 				return m, nil
246: 			}
247: 			if err := instance.SetTitle(string(runes[:len(runes)-1])); err != nil {
248: 				return m, m.handleError(err)
249: 			}
250: 		case tea.KeySpace:
251: 			if err := instance.SetTitle(instance.Title + " "); err != nil {
252: 				return m, m.handleError(err)
253: 			}
254: 		case tea.KeyEsc:
255: 			m.nav.Kill()
256: 			m.state = stateDefault
257: 			m.newInstance = nil
258: 			m.instanceChanged()
259: 
260: 			return m, tea.Sequence(
261: 				tea.WindowSize(),
262: 				func() tea.Msg {
263: 					m.menu.SetState(ui.StateDefault)
264: 					return nil
265: 				},
266: 			)
267: 		default:
268: 		}
269: 		return m, nil
270: 	} else if m.state == statePrompt {
271: 		// Use the new TextInputOverlay component to handle all key events
272: 		shouldClose := m.textInputOverlay.HandleKeyPress(msg)
273: 
274: 		// Check if the form was submitted or canceled
275: 		if shouldClose {
276: 			selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
277: 			// TODO: this should never happen since we set the instance in the previous state.
278: 			if selected == nil {
279: 				return m, nil
280: 			}
281: 			if m.textInputOverlay.IsSubmitted() {
282: 				promptText := m.textInputOverlay.GetValue()
283: 				if err := selected.SendPrompt(promptText); err != nil {
284: 					// TODO: we probably end up in a bad state here.
285: 					return m, m.handleError(err)
286: 				}
287: 				// Emit audit event for prompt sent (truncate to 200 chars).
288: 				msg := promptText
289: 				if len(msg) > 200 {
290: 					msg = msg[:200]
291: 				}
292: 				m.audit(auditlog.EventPromptSent, msg,
293: 					auditlog.WithInstance(selected.Title),
294: 				)
295: 			}
296: 
297: 			// Close the overlay and reset state
298: 			m.textInputOverlay = nil
299: 			m.state = stateDefault
300: 			return m, tea.Sequence(
301: 				tea.WindowSize(),
302: 				func() tea.Msg {
303: 					m.menu.SetState(ui.StateDefault)
304: 					m.showHelpScreen(helpStart(selected), nil)
305: 					return nil
306: 				},
307: 			)
308: 		}
309: 
310: 		return m, nil
311: 	}
312: 
313: 	// Handle PR title input state
314: 	if m.state == statePRTitle {
315: 		if m.textInputOverlay == nil {
316: 			m.state = stateDefault
317: 			return m, nil
318: 		}
319: 		shouldClose := m.textInputOverlay.HandleKeyPress(msg)
320: 		if shouldClose {
321: 			if m.textInputOverlay.IsSubmitted() {
322: 				prTitle := m.textInputOverlay.GetValue()
323: 				selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
324: 				if selected != nil && prTitle != "" {
325: 					m.pendingPRTitle = prTitle
326: 					m.textInputOverlay = nil
327: 
328: 					// Generate a PR body from git data
329: 					generatedBody := ""
330: 					worktree, err := selected.GetGitWorktree()
331: 					if err == nil {
332: 						body, genErr := worktree.GeneratePRBody()
333: 						if genErr == nil {
334: 							generatedBody = body
335: 						}
336: 					}
337: 
338: 					// Transition to PR body editing state
339: 					m.state = statePRBody
340: 					m.textInputOverlay = overlay.NewTextInputOverlay("pr description (edit or submit)", generatedBody)
341: 					m.textInputOverlay.SetSize(80, 20)
342: 					return m, nil
343: 				}
344: 			}
345: 			m.textInputOverlay = nil
346: 			m.state = stateDefault
347: 			m.menu.SetState(ui.StateDefault)
348: 			return m, tea.WindowSize()
349: 		}
350: 		return m, nil
351: 	}
352: 
353: 	// Handle PR body input state
354: 	if m.state == statePRBody {
355: 		if m.textInputOverlay == nil {
356: 			m.state = stateDefault
357: 			return m, nil
358: 		}
359: 		shouldClose := m.textInputOverlay.HandleKeyPress(msg)
360: 		if shouldClose {
361: 			if m.textInputOverlay.IsSubmitted() {
362: 				prBody := m.textInputOverlay.GetValue()
363: 				prTitle := m.pendingPRTitle
364: 				selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
365: 				if selected != nil && prTitle != "" {
366: 					m.textInputOverlay = nil
367: 					m.pendingPRTitle = ""
368: 					m.state = stateDefault
369: 					m.menu.SetState(ui.StateDefault)
370: 					m.pendingPRToastID = m.toastManager.Loading("Creating PR...")
371: 					prToastID := m.pendingPRToastID
372: 					capturedTitle := selected.Title
373: 					capturedPRTitle := prTitle
374: 					return m, tea.Batch(tea.WindowSize(), func() tea.Msg {
375: 						commitMsg := fmt.Sprintf("[kas] update from '%s' on %s", capturedTitle, time.Now().Format(time.RFC822))
376: 						worktree, err := selected.GetGitWorktree()
377: 						if err != nil {
378: 							return prErrorMsg{id: prToastID, err: err}
379: 						}
380: 						if err := worktree.CreatePR(capturedPRTitle, prBody, commitMsg); err != nil {
381: 							return prErrorMsg{id: prToastID, err: err}
382: 						}
383: 						return prCreatedMsg{instanceTitle: capturedTitle, prTitle: capturedPRTitle}
384: 					}, m.toastTickCmd())
385: 				}
386: 			}
387: 			m.textInputOverlay = nil
388: 			m.pendingPRTitle = ""
389: 			m.state = stateDefault
390: 			m.menu.SetState(ui.StateDefault)
391: 			return m, tea.WindowSize()
392: 		}
393: 		return m, nil
394: 	}
395: 
396: 	// Handle instance rename state
397: 	if m.state == stateRenameInstance {
398: 		if m.textInputOverlay == nil {
399: 			m.state = stateDefault
400: 			return m, nil
401: 		}
402: 		shouldClose := m.textInputOverlay.HandleKeyPress(msg)
403: 		if shouldClose {
404: 			if m.textInputOverlay.IsSubmitted() {
405: 				newName := m.textInputOverlay.GetValue()
406: 				selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
407: 				if selected != nil && newName != "" {
408: 					selected.Title = newName
409: 					m.saveAllInstances()
410: 				}
411: 			}
412: 			m.textInputOverlay = nil
413: 			m.state = stateDefault
414: 			m.menu.SetState(ui.StateDefault)
415: 			return m, tea.WindowSize()
416: 		}
417: 		return m, nil
418: 	}
419: 
420: 	// Handle plan rename state
421: 	if m.state == stateRenameTask {
422: 		if m.textInputOverlay == nil {
423: 			m.state = stateDefault
424: 			return m, nil
425: 		}
426: 		shouldClose := m.textInputOverlay.HandleKeyPress(msg)
427: 		if shouldClose {
428: 			if m.textInputOverlay.IsSubmitted() {
429: 				newName := strings.TrimSpace(m.textInputOverlay.GetValue())
430: 				planFile := m.nav.GetSelectedPlanFile()
431: 				if planFile != "" && newName != "" && m.taskState != nil {
432: 					oldFile := planFile
433: 					newFile, err := m.taskState.Rename(oldFile, newName)
434: 					if err != nil {
435: 						m.textInputOverlay = nil
436: 						m.state = stateDefault
437: 						m.menu.SetState(ui.StateDefault)
438: 						return m, m.handleError(err)
439: 					}
440: 					// Update any instances that referenced the old plan file.
441: 					for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
442: 						if inst.TaskFile == oldFile {
443: 							inst.TaskFile = newFile
444: 						}
445: 					}
446: 					for _, inst := range m.allInstances {
447: 						if inst.TaskFile == oldFile {
448: 							inst.TaskFile = newFile
449: 						}
450: 					}
451: 					_ = m.saveAllInstances()
452: 					m.updateSidebarTasks()
453: 					m.nav.SelectByID(ui.SidebarPlanPrefix + newFile)
454: 				}
455: 			}
456: 			m.textInputOverlay = nil
457: 			m.state = stateDefault
458: 			m.menu.SetState(ui.StateDefault)
459: 			return m, tea.WindowSize()
460: 		}
461: 		return m, nil
462: 	}
463: 
464: 	// Handle chat-about-plan question input
465: 	if m.state == stateChatAboutTask {
466: 		if m.textInputOverlay == nil {
467: 			m.state = stateDefault
468: 			return m, nil
469: 		}
470: 		shouldClose := m.textInputOverlay.HandleKeyPress(msg)
471: 		if shouldClose {
472: 			if m.textInputOverlay.IsSubmitted() {
473: 				question := m.textInputOverlay.GetValue()
474: 				planFile := m.pendingChatAboutTask
475: 				m.textInputOverlay = nil
476: 				m.pendingChatAboutTask = ""
477: 				m.state = stateDefault
478: 				m.menu.SetState(ui.StateDefault)
479: 				if planFile != "" && question != "" {
480: 					return m.spawnChatAboutTask(planFile, question)
481: 				}
482: 				return m, tea.WindowSize()
483: 			}
484: 			m.textInputOverlay = nil
485: 			m.pendingChatAboutTask = ""
486: 			m.state = stateDefault
487: 			m.menu.SetState(ui.StateDefault)
488: 			return m, tea.WindowSize()
489: 		}
490: 		return m, nil
491: 	}
492: 
493: 	// Handle focus mode — forward keys directly to the agent's PTY
494: 	if m.state == stateFocusAgent {
495: 		// Ctrl+Space exits focus mode
496: 		if msg.Type == tea.KeyCtrlAt {
497: 			m.exitFocusMode()
498: 			return m, tea.WindowSize()
499: 		}
500: 
501: 		// Ctrl+Up/Down: cycle through active instances (wrapping) while staying in focus mode
502: 		if msg.Type == tea.KeyCtrlUp || msg.Type == tea.KeyCtrlDown {
503: 			if msg.Type == tea.KeyCtrlUp {
504: 				m.nav.CyclePrevActive()
505: 			} else {
506: 				m.nav.CycleNextActive()
507: 			}
508: 			cmd := m.instanceChanged()
509: 			// Re-enter focus mode for the newly selected instance
510: 			focusCmd := m.enterFocusMode()
511: 			return m, tea.Batch(cmd, focusCmd)
512: 		}
513: 
514: 		// Preview tab focus: forward to embedded terminal
515: 		if m.previewTerminal == nil {
516: 			m.exitFocusMode()
517: 			return m, nil
518: 		}
519: 		data := keyToBytes(msg)
520: 		if data == nil {
521: 			return m, nil
522: 		}
523: 		if err := m.previewTerminal.SendKey(data); err != nil {
524: 			return m, m.handleError(err)
525: 		}
526: 		return m, nil
527: 	}
528: 
529: 	// Handle send prompt state
530: 	if m.state == stateSendPrompt {
531: 		if m.textInputOverlay == nil {
532: 			m.state = stateDefault
533: 			return m, nil
534: 		}
535: 		shouldClose := m.textInputOverlay.HandleKeyPress(msg)
536: 		if shouldClose {
537: 			if m.textInputOverlay.IsSubmitted() {
538: 				value := m.textInputOverlay.GetValue()
539: 				selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
540: 				if selected != nil && value != "" {
541: 					if err := selected.SendPrompt(value); err != nil {
542: 						m.textInputOverlay = nil
543: 						m.state = stateDefault
544: 						m.menu.SetState(ui.StateDefault)
545: 						return m, m.handleError(err)
546: 					}
547: 					selected.SetStatus(session.Running)
548: 					// Emit audit event for prompt sent (truncate to 200 chars).
549: 					auditMsg := value
550: 					if len(auditMsg) > 200 {
551: 						auditMsg = auditMsg[:200]
552: 					}
553: 					m.audit(auditlog.EventPromptSent, auditMsg,
554: 						auditlog.WithInstance(selected.Title),
555: 					)
556: 				}
557: 			}
558: 			m.textInputOverlay = nil
559: 			m.state = stateDefault
560: 			m.menu.SetState(ui.StateDefault)
561: 			return m, tea.WindowSize()
562: 		}
563: 		return m, nil
564: 	}
565: 
566: 	// Handle confirmation state
567: 	if m.state == stateConfirm {
568: 		if m.confirmationOverlay == nil {
569: 			m.state = stateDefault
570: 			return m, nil
571: 		}
572: 		switch msg.String() {
573: 		case m.confirmationOverlay.ConfirmKey, "enter":
574: 			action := m.pendingConfirmAction
575: 			m.state = stateDefault
576: 			m.confirmationOverlay = nil
577: 			m.pendingConfirmAction = nil
578: 			m.pendingWaveAbortAction = nil
579: 			m.pendingWaveNextAction = nil
580: 			m.pendingWaveConfirmTaskFile = ""
581: 			// Return the action as a tea.Cmd so bubbletea runs it asynchronously.
582: 			// This prevents blocking the UI during I/O (git push, etc.).
583: 			return m, action
584: 		case "a":
585: 			// 'a' = abort, used by the failed-wave decision dialog.
586: 			abortAction := m.pendingWaveAbortAction
587: 			if abortAction == nil {
588: 				return m, nil // 'a' not bound for this confirm dialog
589: 			}
590: 			m.state = stateDefault
591: 			m.confirmationOverlay = nil
592: 			m.pendingConfirmAction = nil
593: 			m.pendingWaveAbortAction = nil
594: 			m.pendingWaveNextAction = nil
595: 			m.pendingWaveConfirmTaskFile = ""
596: 			return m, abortAction
597: 		case m.confirmationOverlay.CancelKey:
598: 			// If this is the failed-wave dialog and 'n' (next wave) is the cancel key,
599: 			// fire the advance action instead of the normal cancel/re-prompt logic.
600: 			if m.pendingWaveNextAction != nil {
601: 				nextAction := m.pendingWaveNextAction
602: 				m.state = stateDefault
603: 				m.confirmationOverlay = nil
604: 				m.pendingConfirmAction = nil
605: 				m.pendingWaveAbortAction = nil
606: 				m.pendingWaveNextAction = nil
607: 				m.pendingWaveConfirmTaskFile = ""
608: 				return m, nextAction
609: 			}
610: 			// "No" — user explicitly declined.
611: 			// Reset the orchestrator confirm latch when the user cancels a wave prompt,
612: 			// so the prompt can reappear on the next metadata tick (fixes deadlock).
613: 			if m.pendingWaveConfirmTaskFile != "" {
614: 				if orch, ok := m.waveOrchestrators[m.pendingWaveConfirmTaskFile]; ok {
615: 					orch.ResetConfirm()
616: 				}
617: 				m.pendingWaveConfirmTaskFile = ""
618: 			}
619: 			// Planner signal "no": kill planner instance, mark prompted, leave plan ready.
620: 			if m.pendingPlannerTaskFile != "" {
621: 				m.plannerPrompted[m.pendingPlannerTaskFile] = true
622: 				m.killExistingPlanAgent(m.pendingPlannerTaskFile, session.AgentTypePlanner)
623: 				_ = m.saveAllInstances()
624: 				m.updateNavPanelStatus()
625: 				m.pendingPlannerInstanceTitle = ""
626: 				m.pendingPlannerTaskFile = ""
627: 			}
628: 			m.state = stateDefault
629: 			m.confirmationOverlay = nil
630: 			m.pendingConfirmAction = nil
631: 			m.pendingWaveAbortAction = nil
632: 			m.pendingWaveNextAction = nil
633: 			return m, nil
634: 		case "esc":
635: 			// Esc — preserve everything, allow re-prompt on next tick (after cooldown).
636: 			if m.pendingWaveConfirmTaskFile != "" {
637: 				if orch, ok := m.waveOrchestrators[m.pendingWaveConfirmTaskFile]; ok {
638: 					orch.ResetConfirm()
639: 				}
640: 				m.pendingWaveConfirmTaskFile = ""
641: 				m.waveConfirmDismissedAt = time.Now()
642: 			}
643: 			// Planner signal esc: same as cancel — signal is consumed, can't re-trigger.
644: 			if m.pendingPlannerTaskFile != "" {
645: 				m.plannerPrompted[m.pendingPlannerTaskFile] = true
646: 				m.killExistingPlanAgent(m.pendingPlannerTaskFile, session.AgentTypePlanner)
647: 				_ = m.saveAllInstances()
648: 				m.updateNavPanelStatus()
649: 				m.pendingPlannerInstanceTitle = ""
650: 				m.pendingPlannerTaskFile = ""
651: 			}
652: 			m.state = stateDefault
653: 			m.confirmationOverlay = nil
654: 			m.pendingConfirmAction = nil
655: 			m.pendingWaveAbortAction = nil
656: 			m.pendingWaveNextAction = nil
657: 			return m, nil
658: 		default:
659: 			return m, nil
660: 		}
661: 	}
662: 
663: 	// Handle permission prompt state
664: 	if m.state == statePermission {
665: 		if m.permissionOverlay == nil {
666: 			m.state = stateDefault
667: 			return m, nil
668: 		}
669: 		shouldClose := m.permissionOverlay.HandleKeyPress(msg)
670: 		if shouldClose {
671: 			if m.permissionOverlay.IsConfirmed() {
672: 				choice := m.permissionOverlay.Choice()
673: 				// Read the pattern/description from the overlay (captured at detection
674: 				// time) rather than re-parsing CachedContent, which may have changed.
675: 				cacheKey := config.CacheKey(m.permissionOverlay.Pattern(), m.permissionOverlay.Description())
676: 				inst := m.pendingPermissionInstance
677: 
678: 				// Cache "allow always" decisions
679: 				if choice == overlay.PermissionAllowAlways && cacheKey != "" && m.permissionStore != nil {
680: 					m.permissionStore.Remember(m.activeProject(), cacheKey)
681: 				}
682: 
683: 				m.permissionOverlay = nil
684: 				m.state = stateDefault
685: 
686: 				// Guard against re-trigger: the pane still shows the permission
687: 				// prompt for a few ticks while the key sequence propagates.
688: 				// Without this, the next metadata tick re-opens the modal.
689: 				if inst != nil {
690: 					guardKey := cacheKey
691: 					if guardKey == "" {
692: 						guardKey = "__handled__"
693: 					}
694: 					m.permissionHandled[inst] = guardKey
695: 				}
696: 
697: 				if inst != nil {
698: 					// overlay.PermissionChoice and tmux.PermissionChoice share the same
699: 					// iota ordering, so a direct cast is safe.
700: 					capturedInst := inst
701: 					capturedChoice := tmux.PermissionChoice(choice)
702: 					// Emit audit event for permission answered.
703: 					choiceStr := "allow once"
704: 					switch choice {
705: 					case overlay.PermissionAllowAlways:
706: 						choiceStr = "allow always"
707: 					case overlay.PermissionReject:
708: 						choiceStr = "reject"
709: 					}
710: 					m.audit(auditlog.EventPermissionAnswered, choiceStr,
711: 						auditlog.WithInstance(inst.Title),
712: 					)
713: 					m.pendingPermissionInstance = nil
714: 					return m, func() tea.Msg {
715: 						capturedInst.SendPermissionResponse(capturedChoice)
716: 						return nil
717: 					}
718: 				}
719: 			}
720: 			// Esc dismiss — also guard so the same prompt doesn't re-open.
721: 			if m.pendingPermissionInstance != nil {
722: 				guardKey := m.permissionOverlay.Pattern()
723: 				if guardKey == "" {
724: 					guardKey = "__handled__"
725: 				}
726: 				m.permissionHandled[m.pendingPermissionInstance] = guardKey
727: 			}
728: 			m.permissionOverlay = nil
729: 			m.pendingPermissionInstance = nil
730: 			m.state = stateDefault
731: 			return m, nil
732: 		}
733: 		return m, nil
734: 	}
735: 
736: 	// Handle new plan description state
737: 	if m.state == stateNewPlan {
738: 		if m.textInputOverlay == nil {
739: 			m.state = stateDefault
740: 			return m, nil
741: 		}
742: 		shouldClose := m.textInputOverlay.HandleKeyPress(msg)
743: 		if shouldClose {
744: 			if m.textInputOverlay.IsSubmitted() {
745: 				description := strings.TrimSpace(m.textInputOverlay.GetValue())
746: 				if description == "" {
747: 					m.state = stateDefault
748: 					m.menu.SetState(ui.StateDefault)
749: 					m.textInputOverlay = nil
750: 					return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("description cannot be empty"))
751: 				}
752: 				// Set heuristic title as fallback; AI title will replace it when it arrives
753: 				m.pendingPlanName = heuristicPlanTitle(description)
754: 				m.pendingPlanDesc = description
755: 				m.textInputOverlay = nil
756: 
757: 				// If the first line is already a viable slug, skip AI derivation
758: 				if firstLineIsViableSlug(description) {
759: 					topicNames := m.getTopicNames()
760: 					topicNames = append([]string{"(No topic)"}, topicNames...)
